{{Quote|Now we will vanquish any who still hold to his ways... who will not serve the Imperial cause! As the Empire rules the galaxy, Droid World is ours to command!|A Z-X3 unit, inciting rebellion on Kligson's Moon|<ref name="NEGTD" />}}
The '''Z-X3 experimental droid trooper''' was a prototype [[Battle droid/Legends|battle droid]] created by [[Tagge Company/Legends|Tagge Company]] during the era of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. Designed to function in environments too hazardous for organic [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]], the Z-X3 was the brainchild of [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Officer/Legends|officer]] [[Rom Mohc]], who was inspired by his experiences facing [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederate]] battle droids during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]. However, the Empire rejected the droid troopers, leaving the few Z-X3 units that were produced to either find remote Imperial service or fall into private ownership.

==Characteristics==
{{Quote|This kind of product isn't ''worth'' the effort. Most Imperial handiwork ''isn't''—''Zee-Exthree'' here is one of the exceptions.|Kligson|<ref name="marvel47" />}}
Essentially the second generation of the [[Tagge Company/Legends|Tagge Company's]] [[L8-L9]] [[Battle droid/Legends|battle droid]] model, the Z-X3 experimental [[Droid/Legends|droid]] trooper was designed to operate in extreme conditions, ones too hazardous for the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire's]] organic [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]]—specifically, highly [[Radiation/Legends|irradiated]] or [[Gravity/Legends|zero-G]] environments. 1.9 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] tall and [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]], the Z-X3 droids were stylistically modeled after the stormtroopers they were meant to support, with [[Color/Legends|crimson]] plating inspired by [[Stormtrooper armor/Legends|stormtrooper armor]]. The Z-X3 lacked built-in weaponry, instead commonly carrying a [[Blaster rifle/Legends|blaster rifle]] or mounting a [[Briletto Company|Briletto]] [[AAP blaster box|AAP-IV blaster box]] on its chest. The droid also boasted enhanced physical strength, and a high level of intelligence separated from any central [[Computer/Legends|computer]].<ref name="NEGTD" />

==History==
{{Quote|I am an ''experimental model'', designed by Taage Industries to work under extreme planetary conditions even ''stormtroopers'' couldn't survive. The Imperials were not ''satisfied''… I was ''rejected''. Fortunately—Kligson ''reclaimed'' me, and has now given me many ''new'' opportunities.|Z-X3, to [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]]|<ref name="marvel47" />}}
[[File:UppityDroids-MSW47.jpg|thumb|left|180px|Z-X3's mutiny]]
Created and manufactured by the Tagge Company during the era of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], the Z-X3 droid trooper was based off an idea from Imperial [[Officer/Legends|officer]] [[Rom Mohc]], who had served in the [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army of the Republic]] during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] and came to admire the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Separatist]] droid armies. As such, Mohc became fixated on the idea of droid stormtroopers that could provide support to the Empire's organic troops on the ground. Mohc launched the "Droid Trooper Project" in order to bring his idea to fruition, and by the years following the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], he found a willing partner in the Tagge Company—<ref name="NEGTD" /> its [[Baron/Legends|Baron]], [[Orman Tagge/Legends|Orman Tagge]], had remained interested in the concept after the failure of his L8-L9 droid model during the Clone Wars.<ref name="db">{{DB|droid|l8l9|L8-L9}}</ref>

TaggeCo initially produced a crop of less than 100 Z-X3 units, enough for the Empire to test them in a controlled setting. However, by that time, the defeat of the Separatist battle droids was still too fresh in the minds of the Imperial brass, and the project was scrapped. Although Mohc would eventually refine his idea into what became the [[Dark Trooper Project]], the few Z-X3 droids were rejected and slated for recycling. A few found service with the Empire regardless at remote [[Garrison/Legends|garrisons]], but many more made their way into private hands.<ref name="NEGTD" /> One such droid, later simply called [[Z-X3]], found its way to the "Droid World" of [[Kligson's Moon/Legends|Kligson's Moon]], where it became the chief [[Lieutenant/Legends|lieutenant]] of the [[Cyborg/Legends|cyborg]] [[Technician/Legends|technician]] [[Kligson/Legends|Kligson]] while maintaining secret Imperial sympathies. In [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]] Z-X3 incited a [[Kligson's Moon civil war|rebellion]] to seize the Droid World in the Empire's name, but was defeated and destroyed.<ref name="marvel47">[[Star Wars (1977) 47|''Star Wars'' (1977) 47]]</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
The Z-X3 experimental droid trooper first appeared in [[Star Wars (1977) 47|''Star Wars'' (1977) 47]], written by [[Archie Goodwin]] and released in [[1981]].<ref name="marvel47" /> The droid class later received a full backstory, and its full name, when it received an entry in ''[[The New Essential Guide to Droids]]'' in [[2006]].<ref name="NEGTD" />
